column1: # etude — tier registry
# Source of truth for hardware-tier → model recommendations.
# Everything else (detect-tier.sh, install.sh, test.sh, docs) reads from here.
# Format: pipe-separated, one row per (tier, model). Comments start with '#'.
# Leading/trailing whitespace per field is stripped.
# Columns:
#   tier         — must match a config file at config/opencode/<tier>.json
#   model        — ollama tag, exactly as you would pass to `ollama pull`
#   size_gb      — approximate on-disk size, for disk-space preflight and UX
#   context      — recommended context window for this (tier, model) pair
#   reliability  — good | watching | broken
#                    good      : tested and recommended
#                    watching  : not yet ready for agentic use, tracked for promotion
#                    broken    : known-bad, hidden from default picker
#   role         — daily | heavy | speed | alt
#                    daily  : the default pick when the user just wants something that works
#                    heavy  : slower, smarter, close-the-browser mode
#                    speed  : fit-entirely-in-fast-memory mode (GPU full-VRAM pick)
#                    alt    : alternative at the same role, shown in picker
#   note         — one line, decision-aiding (tells the user when to pick this)
# Last reviewed: 2026-04-12
# tier          | model                | size_gb | context | reliability | role  | note
mac-light       | qwen3:4b             | 2.4     | 16384   | good        | daily | Snappy. Tool calling works on simple sequences. Pick this if your Mac is always doing other things.
mac-light       | qwen3:8b             | 5.2     | 16384   | good        | heavy | Better code, more reliable tool calls, slower. Close your browser before heavy sessions on 16GB.
mac-light       | gemma4:e4b           | 5.6     | 16384   | watching    | —     | Capable chat model but tool calling regresses in Ollama 0.20.x. Promote to daily once fixed upstream.
mac-air-24gb    | qwen3:8b             | 5.2     | 32768   | good        | daily | Author's daily driver on M3 Air 24GB. Comfortable with a browser open.
mac-air-24gb    | qwen3-coder:30b-a3b  | 17.0    | 32768   | good        | heavy | MoE with ~3B active per token. Close the browser, focused session only — fanless Air will thermal-bind.
mac-air-24gb    | gemma4:e4b           | 5.6     | 32768   | watching    | —     | See mac-light row. Watch this for promotion.
mac-pro-32gb    | qwen3-coder:30b-a3b  | 17.0    | 65536   | good        | daily | MLX backend activates at 32GB (Ollama 0.19+). This is the sweet-spot tier.
mac-heavy-64gb  | qwen3-coder:30b-a3b  | 17.0    | 65536   | good        | daily | Same model, much more headroom for context and other apps.
mac-heavy-64gb  | qwen3-coder-next:80b | 46.0    | 65536   | watching    | heavy | Larger MoE. Unverified on this tier as of last review.
mac-max         | qwen3-coder-next:80b | 46.0    | 131072  | watching    | daily | Top of what "local-only" means. Unverified.
gpu-light       | qwen3:8b             | 5.2     | 32768   | good        | daily | Fits comfortably in 12GB VRAM. Tight on context above 16K.
gpu-16gb        | qwen3-coder:30b-a3b  | 17.0    | 32768   | good        | daily | Partial offload to system RAM — MoE makes it tolerable. Author's RTX 5080 daily driver.
gpu-16gb        | qwen3:8b             | 5.2     | 32768   | good        | speed | Speed mode, full VRAM at Q8 (~8.5GB). Pick this when you want snappy turnaround.
gpu-24gb        | qwen3-coder:30b-a3b  | 17.0    | 65536   | good        | daily | Fits comfortably in VRAM. Room for longer context than gpu-16gb.
gpu-24gb        | qwen3:32b            | 20.0    | 32768   | watching    | alt   | Dense alternative. Unverified.
gpu-heavy       | qwen3:32b            | 20.0    | 65536   | watching    | daily | Dense 32B or larger MoE. Workstation territory. Unverified.
